content: Supabase/Postgres RLS: policies can be scoped with TO anon / TO authenticated. Public read pattern: CREATE POLICY "..." ON table FOR SELECT TO anon USING (status = 'published'). anon-key-only requests (no session) run as the anon Postgres role; requests with a valid JWT run as authenticated. Existing owner-scoped policies using auth.uid() do NOT grant any rows to anon (auth.uid() is null for anon), so a dedicated additional policy scoped to anon is required for public reads — it does not weaken the existing owner policy since Postgres RLS policies are additive (OR'd together) per operation.
